🏃 Health and Physical Education

Environmental Sanitation

Learn how a clean environment leads to a healthy life!

📚 Unit 2 ⏱️ 8 min read

Unit 2: Environmental Sanitation

Environmental sanitation means keeping our surroundings clean to prevent diseases and maintain good health.

Lesson 1: Environmental Sanitation

What is Environment?

The environment is everything around us – air, water, land, plants, animals, and other people.

What is Environmental Sanitation?

Environmental sanitation is the practice of keeping our surroundings clean, safe, and healthy.

Components of Environmental Sanitation

ComponentWhat it meansExamples
Clean airNo smoke, dust, or harmful gasesPlanting trees, not burning waste
Clean waterSafe drinking waterBoiling water, using filters
Clean landNo garbage or waste on groundProper waste disposal
Clean homeTidy and dust-free houseSweeping, mopping daily
Clean schoolNeat classrooms and groundsPicking up litter
Proper toilet useUsing and maintaining toiletsNot open defecation
DrainageClean water drainsNo clogged or open drains

Why is Environmental Sanitation Important?

ReasonExplanation
Prevents diseasesGerms grow in dirty places
Keeps us healthyClean surroundings = healthy people
Makes places beautifulNo garbage or bad smells
Protects animalsAnimals also need a clean home
Saves the planetLess pollution, better future

How to Maintain Environmental Sanitation

ActionHow to do it
Sweep and mopClean your house and classroom daily
Use a dustbinAlways throw waste in a bin
Clean the toiletUse toilet cleaner and flush after use
Do not spit anywhereSpit only in sinks or toilets
Wash handsAfter using toilet and before eating
Keep drains cleanDo not throw garbage in drains
Plant treesTrees clean the air
Clean your surroundingsParticipate in school cleanliness drives

Waste Management: The 3 R’s

RMeaningExample
ReduceUse lessCarry a cloth bag instead of plastic bags
ReuseUse againUse both sides of paper, refill water bottles
RecycleMake into new thingsSend plastic, glass, and paper to recycling

Types of Waste

TypeMeaningExamplesHow to dispose
BiodegradableDecomposes naturallyVegetable peels, leaves, paperCompost (make manure)
Non-biodegradableDoes not decompose easilyPlastic, glass, metalRecycle or special disposal
HazardousHarmful to healthBatteries, chemicals, medicinesSpecial disposal (do not mix)

💡 Did you know? A plastic bottle takes about 450 years to decompose!

Proper Use of Toilets

Do ✅Don’t ❌
Use a toilet every timePractice open defecation (in fields or open areas)
Flush after usingLeave waste unflushed
Wash hands after usingLeave water on the floor
Keep the toilet dryThrow garbage in the toilet
Report a broken toiletUse a dirty toilet without cleaning

⚠️ Open defecation (going to the toilet in the open) spreads diseases like diarrhea, cholera, and typhoid.

Lesson 2: Health Hazards of Environment Pollution

Pollution is the introduction of harmful substances into the environment. Pollution causes many health problems.

Types of Pollution

TypeCauseEffects on Health
Air PollutionSmoke from factories, vehicles, burning wasteAsthma, lung diseases, cough, eye irritation
Water PollutionDirty water from factories, sewage, chemicalsDiarrhea, typhoid, cholera, skin diseases
Land/Soil PollutionGarbage, plastic, pesticidesInfections, breeding ground for flies and rats
Noise PollutionLoud speakers, vehicles, machinesHearing loss, stress, headache, lack of sleep

Air Pollution

Sources of Air Pollution:

  • Smoke from factories and vehicles
  • Burning of wood, coal, or garbage
  • Dust from construction sites

Health Effects:

Disease/ProblemExplanation
AsthmaDifficulty breathing
BronchitisCough with mucus
Lung cancerLong-term exposure
Eye irritationRed, watery eyes
AllergiesSneezing, runny nose

Water Pollution

Sources of Water Pollution:

  • Industrial waste dumped into rivers
  • Sewage and toilet waste in water
  • Chemical fertilizers and pesticides
  • Plastic and garbage in water bodies

Health Effects:

Disease/ProblemExplanation
DiarrheaLoose, watery stools
CholeraSevere diarrhea, dehydration
TyphoidHigh fever, stomach pain
Hepatitis AYellow skin (jaundice)
Skin diseasesRashes, itching

Land/Soil Pollution

Sources of Land Pollution:

  • Garbage and plastic waste
  • Chemical pesticides in farming
  • Hospital waste
  • Electronic waste (old phones, batteries)

Health Effects:

ProblemExplanation
Breeding of flies and ratsFlies and rats spread diseases
Skin infectionsTouching contaminated soil
Food contaminationCrops grown in polluted soil

Noise Pollution

Sources of Noise Pollution:

  • Loudspeakers and DJ systems
  • Vehicle horns
  • Construction work
  • Factory machines
  • Firecrackers

Health Effects:

ProblemExplanation
Hearing lossLoud noises damage ears
Stress and anxietyConstant noise makes you tense
Lack of sleepCannot sleep due to noise
HeadachesContinuous loud sounds cause pain
Difficulty focusingCannot study or work properly

How to Reduce Pollution

Pollution TypeHow to reduce
Air PollutionPlant trees, use public transport, stop burning waste
Water PollutionDo not throw garbage in rivers, treat factory waste
Land PollutionReduce plastic use, compost biodegradable waste
Noise PollutionUse fewer loudspeakers, avoid honking unnecessarily

Individual Actions to Fight Pollution

ActionHow it helps
Plant a treeTrees absorb carbon dioxide and give oxygen
Walk or cycleLess vehicle smoke
Carry a cloth bagLess plastic waste
Turn off lights and fansSaves electricity (less pollution from power plants)
Say no to crackersReduces air and noise pollution
Throw waste in binsKeeps land clean
Educate othersTell friends and family about pollution

🌍 Remember: Earth is our only home. Keep it clean!

Try It Yourself!

Exercise 1: Fill in the Blanks

  1. Keeping our surroundings clean is called environmental ____________.
  2. The three R’s of waste management are ______, ______, and ______.
  3. ____________ waste decomposes naturally.
  4. ____________ pollution causes hearing loss.
  5. ____________ waste does not decompose easily.

Exercise 2: True or False

  1. Open defecation is a healthy practice. (T/F)
  2. Plastic bottles decompose in one year. (T/F)
  3. Air pollution can cause asthma. (T/F)
  4. Composting is a way to recycle biodegradable waste. (T/F)
  5. Loud music has no effect on health. (T/F)

Exercise 3: Match the Pollution to Its Effect

Pollution TypeHealth Effect
Air pollutiona) Diarrhea
Water pollutionb) Hearing loss
Noise pollutionc) Asthma
Land pollutiond) Breeding of flies and rats

Exercise 4: What Type of Waste?

Write “Biodegradable” or “Non-biodegradable”.

  1. Vegetable peels ______
  2. Plastic bottle ______
  3. Paper ______
  4. Glass ______
  5. Leaves ______

Answer Key

Exercise 1: 1. sanitation, 2. Reduce, Reuse, Recycle; 3. Biodegradable, 4. Noise, 5. Non-biodegradable

Exercise 2: 1. F, 2. F, 3. T, 4. T, 5. F

Exercise 3: Air→c, Water→a, Noise→b, Land→d

Exercise 4: 1. Biodegradable, 2. Non-biodegradable, 3. Biodegradable, 4. Non-biodegradable, 5. Biodegradable

Great work! A clean environment means a healthy life. Be a sanitation hero!
